title: Jamaican Goat Stew
description: A flavorful and hearty stew from the Caribbean, made with tender goat meat and a blend of aromatic spices.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the bacon and cook until crispy, then remove and set aside.
Add the goat to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, garlic, thyme, allspice, and scotch bonnet pepper (if using) to the pot.
Cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the tomato paste to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the stew, bacon, and enough stock to cover the goat.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the goat is tender, about 2 hours.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh thyme, if desired.
